fragment: The radiator's fist-knock — two quick hits then a groan, every evening at six, the sound that meant the flat was getting warm and something was awake inside the wall
trigger: Any sudden knocking sound, central heating turning on, pipes groaning in Babička's house

fragment: Head & Shoulders, the blue bottle, sharp and chemical and male — the smell that meant Dad was leaving
trigger: Shampoo aisles in drugstores, the specific blue bottle on any bathroom shelf, men's shower gel in enclosed spaces

fragment: Mum's arms — too tight, the breath held, the heart going too fast under her ear, holding like Anna might evaporate
trigger: Being held by anyone who's anxious, feeling someone's heartbeat when they're upset

fragment: The Czech šššš — Mum's hush, softer than English shhh, drawn out, worried-sounding even when it was supposed to be soothing
trigger: Anyone hushing her, the sound of breath through teeth, the start of a sibilant Czech word

fragment: Dad's chest vibrating low when he sang — 'Yellow Submarine,' the rumble she felt before she heard it
trigger: Low male voices, feeling someone's chest when they speak or sing, bass notes in music

fragment: Damp — the bathroom wall, the growing spot near the toilet, like Babička's basement but wrong because it was upstairs and spreading
trigger: Musty smells, basements, any dark patch on a wall or ceiling

fragment: McVitie's Digestives — the first biscuit she reached for, the taste of 'more,' crumbly and plain and connected to Dad's laugh
trigger: Plain biscuits, anything crumbly, the specific McVitie's wrapper sound

fragment: The Henry hoover starting up — the roar that didn't match the smile, the sound that meant run
trigger: Vacuum cleaners, any motor sound that starts suddenly, appliances with faces

fragment: Pavement cracks from Dad's shoulders — veins, rivers, a map of a world she couldn't see from the ground
trigger: Being picked up, looking down at pavement, the view from any height

fragment: Markéta — yeast and lavender and bread dough and koláče in the tin, the smell of someone who wasn't frightened
trigger: Fresh bread, any Czech pastry, lavender, the specific warmth of a kitchen where someone is baking

fragment: Proboha — Klára's voice hitting the fridge, the word Anna thought was English, the word that meant Mum was tired or the fridge was there or something was wrong
trigger: Czech swear words, anyone bumping into furniture, the sound of exasperation in any language

fragment: The X Factor blue light flickering in the sitting room — Mum watching, Anna asleep on her chest, the only time the arms weren't too tight
trigger: Television light in dark rooms, talent shows, the specific blue glow of a screen on skin

fragment: Peppa Pig — 'I'm Peppa Pig,' 'muddy puddles,' 'dinosaur' — the incantations, the safety rhythm, the words that made the world behave
trigger: Hearing the Peppa Pig theme, any British children's TV from that era, the word 'dinosaur' said in a silly voice

fragment: Nursery — bananas and disinfectant, the smell that meant Mum was leaving and the anxious arms were gone and the hours of waiting were starting
trigger: Disinfectant, overripe bananas, any institutional smell, the specific aroma of primary school corridors

fragment: The fish tank at Bright Horizons — the light, the slow movement, things that didn't ask her to be anything
trigger: Fish tanks, any contained quiet thing, blue light through water

fragment: Mum crying in the car through the nursery window — shoulders shaking, alone, silent — the first time she understood that leaving hurts the leaver
trigger: Seeing anyone cry alone, car parks, the view through windows into private grief

fragment: The blue-black stone — smooth, cool, fitting in her palm, the specific weight of a thing that was hers before it wasn't
trigger: Finding smooth stones, anything that fits perfectly in the palm, the impulse to pocket small beautiful things

fragment: The damp spot shaped like a face if you looked right — growing, spreading, the bathroom wall watching her back
trigger: Water stains, mold patches, any mark on a wall that could be a face if you squinted

fragment: The tap running in the bathroom — Mum's cover sound, the noise that meant crying was happening on the other side of the door
trigger: Taps running in other rooms, the sound of water covering sounds, any bathroom door that's closed too long

fragment: The wallpaper curl by the bathroom door — she picked at it while listening, the flaking edge, the evidence she'd stood there
trigger: Peeling wallpaper, any wall texture near a door, the impulse to pick at things while anxious

fragment: Nando's — peri-peri and chips and the booth by the window, the smell that meant Sunday and Dad and too much food
trigger: Any Nando's, the smell of grilled chicken, the specific red of the sauce bottles

fragment: Sainsbury's café booth by the window — the magazine, Mum's face looser than usual, the only Saturday-morning peace
trigger: Supermarket cafés, magazine racks, any booth by a window where the light is grey and calm

fragment: The Ladbrokes door slamming below — the vibration through the floor, the betting shop sounds that belonged to the flat whether she wanted them to or not
trigger: Doors slamming in buildings with thin floors, any sound that comes through from below

fragment: The burn mark on the curtain edge — she touched it once, the crispy fabric, the evidence that the flat had almost been on fire once or the iron had gotten too close
trigger: Burn marks on fabric, ironing, the smell of scorched material

fragment: The key under the mat — Markéta's arrival sound, the door clicking, Czech starting before the door was shut
trigger: Keys in locks, the sound of someone letting themselves in, Czech spoken in doorways

fragment: The Mothercare baby bath — the blue plastic, the water Dad tested on his elbow, the smallness of it
trigger: Baby baths, the specific blue of cheap plastic, seeing anyone test water temperature

fragment: The Ladbrokes smell from downstairs — betting shop, smoke, men — the smell that came up through the floor and meant the flat wasn't just theirs
trigger: Betting shops, cigarette smoke drifting from anywhere, the smell of places men gather

fragment: The front door opening at 6pm — Dad's sound, the relief, the low rumble of 'alright?' before she could see him
trigger: Front doors opening, the specific sound of a key in a lock at the end of a day
